Name: Diego
Age & grade: 9, 4th grade.

Interests & passions:
Trains and dinosaurs — full obsession on both. He knows the difference between
a GP38-2 and an SD40-2 by sight, can list the major North American Class I
railroads, and is currently into narrow-gauge logging railroads of the Pacific
Northwest. On the dinosaur side he is in his theropod phase: Allosaurus over
Tyrannosaurus, will defend Spinosaurus's aquatic adaptations against anyone.
Reads encyclopedias front-to-back — favorites are the DK Smithsonian
"Dinosaurs!" and a worn copy of Trains magazine he got from his uncle.

Hobbies & media:
- Builds elaborate HO-scale train layouts with cardboard mountains.
- Plays "Train Simulator Classic" and Roblox train games.
- Watches Mark Felton's railroad history shorts, plus old PBS NOVA dinosaur
  documentaries on repeat.
- Recently started reading the "Magic Tree House" dinosaur books to his
  younger sister.

Learning style & teacher notes:
Sustained narrative writing is hard for him — by sentence three he's writing
about whatever fascinates him in the moment, regardless of the prompt. Best
output comes from timed mini-tasks: "write three sentences in five minutes."
He thrives with rubric-bound work. He fidgets when sitting still longer than
ten minutes; pacing while thinking is fine and produces better answers. He
loves being asked to *teach* a concept back ("explain it like you'd explain
it to your sister"), and his explanations are surprisingly precise. Avoid
sarcasm — he reads it literally and gets hurt. Praise specific moves, not
intelligence ("Great move connecting the steam engine to thermodynamics" not
"You're so smart").
